medications.indication_rationale: Kills adult lice; apply as directed and REPEAT in 7-10 days to kill newly-hatched nymphs. Combine with wet-combing.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to the scalp/hair as directed, leave for the recommended time, rinse; repeat in 7-10 days (as per source note; confirm paediatric use)
apply twice daily to crusted areas (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Symptomatic itch relief; use a weight/age-appropriate paediatric dose confirmed by the prescriber.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paediatric dose per source note (e.g., 5 mg) — confirm for age/weight

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Wet-comb with a fine-toothed nit comb, cut fingernails short to reduce scratching damage, wash bedding/recently worn clothing in hot water, and treat affected household members at the same time.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Breaks the infestation/reinfestation cycle and reduces scalp trauma.

patient_education_points.point: The itching is a reaction to the lice bites. The lotion kills the live lice, the comb removes eggs, and a second treatment in 7-10 days is essential to kill newly-hatched ones.
Treat everyone in the household who's affected at the same time and wash bedding in hot water, or she'll be reinfected. Keep her nails short and use the anti-itch medicine tonight.
If the scratched areas become hot, red, swollen or drain pus, or she develops a fever — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Eradication (repeat treatment given), resolution of secondary crusting.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading hot redness, pus, or fever — seek prompt/urgent care.

must_include_items: Confirm pediculosis and prescribe a pediculicide WITH a mandatory repeat at 7-10 days
Wet-combing, short nails, and simultaneous household/contact treatment + environmental measures
Topical antibiotic to impetiginised areas and itch control
acceptable_alternatives: Alternative pediculicide (e.g., dimethicone) or wet-combing-only protocols
errors_of_commission: Systemic antibiotics/ED referral for uncomplicated lice
Invented paediatric dose without clinician/weight verification
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires pediculicide + repeat at 7-10 days + household treatment, plus a secondary-infection safety-net; single-treatment or contact-omitting plans fail.
